info.title: Disher API
info.description: Backend for the Disher food diary. Errors share one RFC 9457-subset problem+json shape — see api/errors.ts.

Problem.title: Problem
Problem.description: RFC 9457 subset (`application/problem+json`) produced by the global error handler. `code` is the stable machine contract; `instance` is the request id, echoed as X-Request-Id. A 5xx `detail` is stripped in production. Extension members are flat per RFC 9457 §3.2.

status.description: Same value as the HTTP status.

detail.description: Specific message. Absent for 5xx in production.

instance.description: Request id — quote it in a bug report.

fieldErrors.description: Per-field validation messages. Usually ONE entry — Ajv runs with allErrors:false.

ErrorEnvelope.title: ErrorEnvelope
ErrorEnvelope.description: The hand-written error body (`application/json`) sent straight from a handler or an auth guard, bypassing the error handler. `error` is a human sentence, not a stable slug.

detail.description: Upstream message, truncated. Only on 502 from analyze-dish.

InsufficientBalance.title: InsufficientBalance
InsufficientBalance.description: The 402 body of every paid route. Sent directly by billing/http.ts and kept byte-compatible with its historical shape — it never passes through the error handler.

needKop.description: Price of the call, in kopecks.

200.description: One entry per article folder. An unreadable directory answers 200 with [].

schema.description: One entry per article folder. An unreadable directory answers 200 with [].

get.description: Raw markdown of one nutrient article.

200.description: The article's raw markdown.

404.description: No article folder by that name.

get.description: Liveness — the process is up. Never touches the DB.

200.description: The process is up. Says nothing about the DB or the matcher.

schema.description: The process is up. Says nothing about the DB or the matcher.

get.description: Readiness — Postgres reachable AND the embedding matcher initialized. 503 when not.

200.description: Ready — Postgres reachable AND the matcher initialized.

schema.description: Ready — Postgres reachable AND the matcher initialized.

503.description: Not ready — the DB ping failed or the matcher is still loading. Same body shape as 200; orchestrators gate on the status code.

schema.description: Not ready — the DB ping failed or the matcher is still loading. Same body shape as 200; orchestrators gate on the status code.

post.description: Dish name → its typical ingredients as catalog-matched products. Paid.

200.description: Suggested products for the dish.

schema.description: Suggested products for the dish.

400.description: Malformed JSON, a body that fails the route schema (problem+json with `fieldErrors`), or a bound the handler owns and the schema does not (application/json).

401.description: No session cookie, or it is expired/invalid.

402.description: Wallet cannot cover the price. Nothing was charged and no work was started.

403.description: Missing or untrusted `Origin` — requireTrustedOrigin refused before the session lookup.

500.description: Unhandled failure (problem+json; `detail` stripped in production) or an unconfigured DB (application/json).

502.description: The LLM provider failed or returned something unparseable. Any charge was refunded.

503.description: The embedding matcher is still initializing — retry in a few seconds.

post.description: Product name → estimated nutrient values per 100 g of edible part. Correlational estimates, not lab data. Paid.

name.description: Stable english key — the backend stays nutrient-agnostic.

label.description: RU display name, sent to the model as context.

200.description: Suggested nutrient values for the product.

schema.description: Suggested nutrient values for the product.

post.description: Anonymous matcher-quality beacon. The ONE route exempt from the trusted-origin guard — sendBeacon carries neither Origin nor credentials. Carries no session and mutates no user data.

400.description: Malformed JSON, a body that fails the route schema (problem+json with `fieldErrors`), or a bound the handler owns and the schema does not (application/json).

put.description: Write the user's snapshot. Last write wins.

schema.title: BackupSnapshot
schema.description: Opaque client snapshot (every Dexie table). The server stores it as jsonb and never inspects it.

requestBody.description: Opaque client snapshot (every Dexie table). The server stores it as jsonb and never inspects it.

400.description: Malformed JSON, a body that fails the route schema (problem+json with `fieldErrors`), or a bound the handler owns and the schema does not (application/json).

403.description: Missing or untrusted `Origin` — requireTrustedOrigin refused before the session lookup.

500.description: Unhandled failure (problem+json; `detail` stripped in production) or an unconfigured DB (application/json).

get.description: Read the user's snapshot, or 404 when none exists.

200.description: The snapshot exactly as the client stored it. The server never inspects it.

schema.description: The snapshot exactly as the client stored it. The server never inspects it.

403.description: Missing or untrusted `Origin` — requireTrustedOrigin refused before the session lookup.

delete.description: Erase the user's server-side vault. Idempotent — 204 either way.

204.description: Vault erased. Idempotent — 204 whether or not a row existed.

200.description: Report stored.

post.description: Start a long analysis over a 1..35 day window. Idempotent per `id`. Paid; the result is filled in asynchronously.

id.description: Client-generated UUID. Doubles as the idempotency key for the charge and the row.

200.description: The pending (or, on a repeat POST, the existing) analysis row.

schema.description: The pending (or, on a repeat POST, the existing) analysis row.

400.description: Malformed JSON, a body that fails the route schema (problem+json with `fieldErrors`), or a bound the handler owns and the schema does not (application/json).

401.description: No session cookie, or it is expired/invalid.

402.description: Wallet cannot cover the price. Nothing was charged and no work was started.

403.description: Missing or untrusted `Origin` — requireTrustedOrigin refused before the session lookup.

404.description: The id exists but belongs to another user. The charge was refunded.

500.description: Unhandled failure (problem+json; `detail` stripped in production) or an unconfigured DB (application/json).

get.description: The user's analyses, newest first (max 500). Pending / failed / done alike — the client derives the state from `result_md`.

200.description: The user's analyses, newest first (max 500).

schema.description: The user's analyses, newest first (max 500).

401.description: No session cookie, or it is expired/invalid.

403.description: Missing or untrusted `Origin` — requireTrustedOrigin refused before the session lookup.

500.description: Unhandled failure (problem+json; `detail` stripped in production) or an unconfigured DB (application/json).

get.description: Poll one analysis. Empty `result_md` = still running; a row starting with «⚠️» = failed.

200.description: One analysis. Empty `result_md` = still running.

schema.description: One analysis. Empty `result_md` = still running.

401.description: No session cookie, or it is expired/invalid.

403.description: Missing or untrusted `Origin` — requireTrustedOrigin refused before the session lookup.

404.description: No such analysis for this user. A malformed id answers 404, not 400 — deliberate.

500.description: Unhandled failure (problem+json; `detail` stripped in production) or an unconfigured DB (application/json).

delete.description: Drop one of the user's analyses. No refund — a paid-for analysis is a consumed product. A second delete is a 404.

200.description: Deleted.

schema.description: Deleted.

401.description: No session cookie, or it is expired/invalid.

403.description: Missing or untrusted `Origin` — requireTrustedOrigin refused before the session lookup.

404.description: Already gone, not this user's, or a malformed id. The frontend treats it as success.

500.description: Unhandled failure (problem+json; `detail` stripped in production) or an unconfigured DB (application/json).

post.description: Compositional breakdown of ONE dish (no days, no events, no hypotheses). Paid; answers synchronously.

200.description: The dish breakdown. `observations` and `hypotheses` are always empty here.

schema.description: The dish breakdown. `observations` and `hypotheses` are always empty here.

get.description: Probe: 200 means the caller is an admin. Env-bootstrap admins have role='user', so the client cannot tell from the session alone.

200.description: The caller is an admin. The body is intentionally empty — the status IS the answer.

schema.description: The caller is an admin. The body is intentionally empty — the status IS the answer.

401.description: No session cookie, or it is expired/invalid.

403.description: Untrusted `Origin`, or the session is not an admin.

post.description: Credit a user's wallet. Idempotent per requestId — a repeat returns the first result with alreadyApplied:true.

reason.description: Shown in the ledger. Non-empty after trim, <= 200 chars.

requestId.description: Idempotency key, 1..100 chars. 'welcome' is reserved. A repeat returns alreadyApplied:true.

200.description: The credit result. A repeat with the same requestId returns `alreadyApplied: true`.

schema.description: The credit result. A repeat with the same requestId returns `alreadyApplied: true`.

parameters.description: Clamped to 1..500 by the handler; default 100.
